112251.explanation: In Diemen, residents are involved actively and in a timely manner in important issues. GroenLinks-PvdA lowers barriers to participation and encourages involvement. An advisory referendum is an additional instrument to let residents express their opinion on important decisions.

112252.explanation: Residents know very well what is needed in their neighborhood. That is why we support self-management, for example of public gardens, where possible.

112256.explanation: New distribution centers take up a lot of space, cause extra traffic, and provide little local added value. That scarce space is desperately needed for housing, greenery, and sustainable or local businesses.

112258.explanation: Diemen does not yet have an asylum seeker center (AZC). The COA is preparing its opening in 2027. GroenLinks-PvdA believes that every municipality must contribute to asylum reception. Therefore, we support an AZC in Diemen, with humane and well-organized reception, in good harmony with residents.

112260.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants clean air and a healthy living environment. Wood burning can cause nuisance and health problems, but a complete ban goes too far. We focus on discouragement, good information, and cleaner alternatives.

112262.explanation: Fireworks are harmful to humans, animals, and the environment. A fireworks show is no longer appropriate for these times. GroenLinks-PvdA believes the municipality should investigate an alternative New Year's Eve show.

112264.explanation: Festivals and parties are important moments for our residents to meet each other. At the same time, the environment must be taken into account. If it appears there is too much noise nuisance, the requirements can be tightened.

112268.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA warmly supports the local broadcaster. If there is reason, the broadcaster can receive extra support.

112269.explanation: It is important that school buildings are of good quality. In Diemen, this is generally the case. However, the building of the Sint-Petrusschool can be improved. We are allocating funds for this.

112273.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses safety and livability. Community officials (BOAs) are important for visibility and addressing nuisance, but more BOAs are not always the solution. We opt for a broad approach with prevention, strong neighborhoods, and enforcement where necessary. If needed, we will provide extra enforcement, but at this moment that is not necessary.

112274.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA stands for safety and privacy. Camera surveillance can sometimes help, but it is not a standard solution. We choose a broad approach with prevention, strong neighborhoods, and only careful use of cameras where appropriate.

112275.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be helped well and quickly. Diemen does this in collaboration with the region. The facilities are good. Therefore, there is no need for a local women's shelter in Diemen.

112278.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that people can live suitably. Smaller rental and owner-occupied homes for the elderly can promote housing flow. We build these homes where possible.

112280.explanation: Social assistance is a right, and therefore we do not require a mandatory counter-performance in exchange. We help residents with a social assistance benefit towards work or daytime activities.

112282.explanation: Diemen has generous policies for residents who are financially struggling. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to maintain this policy.

112284.explanation: There are more and more electric cars, so it is important to expand the number of charging points as well.

112285.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports lowering the maximum speed limit within built-up areas, because this increases safety for (vulnerable) road users.

112286.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that people receive care that suits them. Where possible, there should be room to choose a care provider that aligns with one's faith, culture, or beliefs. At the same time, good care also requires feasibility and availability.

112287.explanation: In youth care, the quality of care must be leading, not the lowest price. Children and families have the right to appropriate and reliable support. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses care that works, with attention to professionals, continuity, and the child's well-being.

112290.explanation: Tourists use Diemen's facilities. A fee may be charged for this. Therefore, the tourist tax remains in place.

112292.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that status holders can make a good start in the Netherlands. Housing is part of this. Therefore, we maintain priority for status holders on social rental homes.

112294.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers the transition to sustainable energy very important. That is why we support solar panels on or around agricultural land.

112296.explanation: Diemen has many good sports facilities that are easily accessible from all of Diemen. Therefore, a new sports park in Diemen-South is not necessary.

112298.explanation: If an initiator comes forward for a coffee shop and can comply with all rules, GroenLinks-PvdA wants to cooperate.

112301.explanation: Shared housing is a first step for many people to live independently. That is why GroenLinks-PvdA wants to allow room rentals under conditions.

112302.explanation: In Diemen, there are already many places where people can meet. GroenLinks-PvdA finds local meeting places important and supports additional neighborhood rooms where there is a need. This strengthens neighborhood cohesion.

112303.explanation: Paid parking must be cost-covering. This is currently the case. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA does not want to reduce the costs.

112304.explanation: There are many parking spaces in the city center. GroenLinks-PvdA does not see the need to increase this number.

112306.explanation: The renovation of the town hall is already proceeding in a sustainable way. GroenLinks-PvdA is proud of this. The plans are being implemented and it is not logical to add extra funds now. We will continue to focus on sustainability in future projects.

112307.explanation: The Zilvermeeuwpad is well lit. If any lighting is broken, it should be repaired as soon as possible.

112251.explanation: Agreed, but preferably only after consultation in neighborhood or district councils.

112256.explanation: As a Randstad municipality, this is a source of economic activity.

112258.explanation: Preferably not, but there is a spreading law, so that is not realistic.

112260.explanation: There should be an injunction including a ban on burning wood.

112262.explanation: As an alternative to illegal fireworks.

112264.explanation: Definitely necessary due to hearing damage concerns.

112268.explanation: They already receive subsidies, so additional money is not needed.

112269.explanation: In the context of clean air in schools, it is necessary. The schools in Diemen already meet the standard. Less CO2 in the classroom leads to better school results.

112273.explanation: Definitely needed if there is also an asylum seekers’ center and further enforcement tasks.

112274.explanation: Despite privacy concerns, a female faction member said 'better filmed than raped.'

112275.explanation: According to us, it does not yet exist.

112278.explanation: There must be more affordable housing for both old and young.

112280.explanation: Not always possible. A mother with children or a disability may limit this.

112282.explanation: The municipality must pay more attention to disposable income.

112284.explanation: Only if the residents request it.

112285.explanation: Only in neighborhoods and districts, not on main thoroughfares.

112286.explanation: Every person is equal to us, regardless of religion, orientation, origin, or color.

112290.explanation: Should be the same as in surrounding municipalities.

112294.explanation: Then the meadow landscape disappears, along with nitrogen absorption and cooling in summer.

112296.explanation: Where then? Better for the Diemerbos.

112298.explanation: Amsterdam is nearby, so there are plenty of options.

112301.explanation: There is already a campus and there is a shortage of affordable housing.

112304.explanation: There are no parking problems in the city center.

112307.explanation: For the feeling of safety, that would be better.

112251.explanation: According to D66 Diemen, residents should be able to have input on decisions. By involving different voices and viewpoints from the start, the final decision will be better. For very important and major decisions, a referendum is a good way to hear all voices in Diemen.

112252.explanation: D66 Diemen welcomes it when residents or entrepreneurs develop their own initiatives. We want the municipality to financially and practically support promising initiatives. For example, taking a green strip into own management to create a butterfly haven or community garden.

112256.explanation: This is not currently an issue in Diemen. Diemen has several smaller business parks. D66 finds it important to preserve these business parks for creative enterprises and smaller manufacturing industries.

112258.explanation: D66 Diemen believes that all municipalities must take responsibility for sheltering refugees. We cannot let people sleep on the grass in Ter Apel. Therefore, we have voted in favor of the arrival of an asylum seekers’ center in Diemen from 2027.

112260.explanation: D66 considers clean air very important. At the same time, we do not want the government to intrude unnecessarily. We do want to inform residents about the disadvantages of burning and when burning can cause nuisance for neighbors.

112262.explanation: D66 is in favor of a fireworks or light show organized by the municipality. This way, residents can enjoy a safe and festive New Year's Eve together, without risks to people, animals, and the environment.

112264.explanation: Events create opportunities for meeting and connection. They are mainly organized by volunteers in Diemen. Therefore, we must help initiators as much as possible with organizing events within all rules and regulations. We look together with them for ways that something can be done.

112268.explanation: Local news creates connection within the community. The local volunteers of Studio DMN are indispensable. Thanks in part to D66 Diemen, they receive a small subsidy every year to continue their work.

112269.explanation: Good education is only possible in good school buildings. Thanks in part to D66 Diemen, a plan has been made and funds allocated for the maintenance of all Diemen schools, now and in the future.

112273.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) are the face of the municipality in the neighborhood. They ensure that residents feel safe in their area, and their presence acts preventively. Diemen is growing. D66 wants the deployment of BOAs and police to increase proportionally with Diemen's population.

112274.explanation: In Diemen, camera surveillance is already used when necessary. There are strict rules for this—to protect residents' privacy and because camera surveillance often merely displaces crime. D66 Diemen believes the current rules regarding camera surveillance are adequate.

112275.explanation: Domestic violence is an urgent problem that often remains hidden. In our region, there must be low-threshold reporting points where victims and bystanders can safely share their concerns. There must also be enough places in women's shelters. This should be available in Diemen and elsewhere in the region.

112278.explanation: D66 Diemen believes everyone has the right to a suitable and affordable home. Building homes for the elderly will free up larger homes for others. Diemen faces a significant shortage of small affordable owner-occupied homes and mid-priced rental homes.

112280.explanation: Residents with temporary no income can rely on social assistance, which prevents extreme poverty. The municipality must help residents going through difficult times to find a job. If that (still) does not succeed, we encourage volunteer work, but do not make it mandatory.

112282.explanation: Fortunately, Diemen has a generous poverty policy, partly thanks to D66. Compared to other municipalities, Diemen has a high income threshold to qualify for assistance. This means we help more residents than other municipalities. D66 wants to maintain this, but not expand it further.

112284.explanation: More and more Diemen residents are driving electric vehicles, and that number is growing rapidly. D66 Diemen therefore wants more public charging stations in Diemen, also in residential streets, and to provide the opportunity to safely charge an electric car with self-generated electricity on a public parking spot.

112285.explanation: D66 is committed to a safe traffic environment. A speed limit of 30 km/h will become the standard. This reduces accidents, noise pollution, and emissions, and increases safety in residential areas. For Diemen's major through roads, an exception will be made and the speed remains 50 km/h.

112286.explanation: The Social Support Act (Wmo) states that residents of Diemen can turn to the municipality for aids or household help. With this assistance, they can participate in society. We trust our care providers to help everyone well, regardless of faith or belief.

112287.explanation: Every young person who needs care must receive it. Currently, too many young people are waiting for help. Unfortunately, funding from the national government is still far from sufficient and there are staff shortages. When purchasing care, quality, reliability, and price must therefore be considered.

112290.explanation: Diemen's finances are in order. Therefore, D66 does not want to increase taxes now. In these uncertain times, unfortunately, there is also no room to lower taxes. That is the honest truth. Only with a balanced budget can Diemen remain a strong and independent municipality.

112292.explanation: Diemen wants to offer status holders a warm welcome. We also have a legal obligation to house a certain number of people each year. Because these people have fled, they have not accumulated waiting time with Woningnet. Without priority, we cannot meet the legal obligation.

112294.explanation: D66 strives to generate the electricity consumption of Diemen households within Diemen's territory. We want solar panels on roofs and preferably not on land, as that takes up too much space. If the land cannot be used otherwise (for example, right next to a railroad or highway), it may be allowed.

112296.explanation: Sport promotes relaxation, health, and meeting others. Thanks in part to D66, a new sports hall has been realized, the sports park has been renewed, and a new sports center called Duran will be built in Diemen Zuid. For a possible future expansion of the sports park, we are looking at the meadow triangle.

112298.explanation: D66 Diemen thinks it is a good idea if soft drugs are fully legalized nationwide. As long as this is not yet the case, we do not want to allow any coffee shops in Diemen.

112301.explanation: D66 Diemen wants to use existing homes more efficiently. We facilitate various forms of shared housing (subletting, friends contracts) and splitting homes. We carefully consider livability and noise nuisance so that shared housing becomes possible, but the neighborhood remains pleasant.

112302.explanation: Currently, there are several community centers where fun free activities are organized for both young and old. It is important to maintain this. If there is a need for an additional community center in a neighborhood, D66 Diemen is positive about that.

112303.explanation: D66 Diemen believes the municipality should not make a profit from parking, but also that other citizens should not pay for someone else's car. Therefore, parking fees should be as cost-covering as possible. At present, this is the case.

112304.explanation: Parking must remain practical and affordable. Residents should be able to park their car nearby. But space is scarce in Diemen. If it gets too crowded, no extra parking spaces will be added, but a blue zone (time-limited parking). Paid parking remains limited to busy areas and should not be a source of income.

112306.explanation: The renovation of the town hall is an inspiring example because it was approached sustainably and circularly. Materials were reused as much as possible. This shows that innovation, sustainability, and economic growth can go hand in hand. No additional funding is needed for this.

112307.explanation: A safe design of public spaces is the first step towards less crime. On the streets, D66 Diemen wants well-lit streets, clear squares, and safe walking routes. If residents report unsafe situations, these must be addressed quickly.

112251.explanation: The municipal council represents the residents in Diemen. Residents' input remains possible at all times and is very valuable. A referendum can delay decision-making.

112252.explanation: The Diemen VVD believes that the management of a sports hall or park should be considered on a case-by-case basis.

112256.explanation: The Diemen VVD gives entrepreneurs room to do business. Companies—small and large—ensure employment in Diemen.

112258.explanation: The Diemen VVD is not fundamentally against an asylum seekers' center (AZC), but can only support it under the right conditions. We voted against the establishment of an AZC at Diemerhof 2 due to the size of the AZC, the duration of the permit, and the need to guarantee safety.

112260.explanation: The Diemen VVD believes that other measures, such as education about stoves or replacing old heaters, can also contribute to cleaner air.

112262.explanation: The Diemen VVD supports an organized fireworks show. This can bring people together and limit nuisance in other places.

112264.explanation: The Diemen VVD wants to work towards a livelier Diemen. Noise standards are necessary, but there is currently no reason to make them stricter.

112268.explanation: The Diemen VVD considers local media important. At this moment, we see no reason for more support through extra funding.

112269.explanation: The Diemen VVD values good housing and quality education. It is not necessary to improve all school buildings.

112273.explanation: The Diemen VVD believes that everyone should feel safe in Diemen—at home, on the street, at school, and in the neighborhood. Everyone, including women, must always be able to walk safely outside, even at night. More enforcement and camera surveillance contribute to this.

112274.explanation: The Diemen VVD believes everyone should feel safe in Diemen—at home, on the street, at school, and in the neighborhood. Together with residents, we identify places that are perceived as unsafe and where (temporary) extra camera surveillance is needed.

112275.explanation: The Diemen VVD stands up for the safety of people without a safe home. If a women's shelter is needed in the region, it can be considered whether this will be realized in Diemen or elsewhere.

112278.explanation: The Diemen VVD supports stimulating housing turnover and building, building, building. It is important, however, that these are suitable homes, also for the elderly target group.

112280.explanation: The Diemen VVD finds participation in society important. Work provides not only income but also an active and social life. We support residents who need this and help as many people as possible find work.

112282.explanation: The Diemen VVD finds it important to offer help to people who need extra support. Regarding the income threshold for poverty policy, this should be reviewed per regulation.

112284.explanation: The Diemen VVD wants more parking spaces. Whether this applies to electric cars or other motor vehicles must be considered on a case-by-case basis.

112285.explanation: The Diemen VVD wants more attention for traffic safety. Reducing speed to 30 km/h should be considered per situation.

112286.explanation: The Diemen VVD supports care for those who need it. We mainly allocate professionals, time, and resources to those who really need it. This calls for a targeted and efficient deployment of social services.

112287.explanation: The Diemen VVD supports youth care for those who truly need it. This requires a targeted and efficient deployment of social services.

112290.explanation: We keep taxes and levies as low as possible to ensure life remains affordable for our residents. The Diemen VVD wants to work towards a livelier Diemen.

112292.explanation: The Diemen VVD wants to utilize all possibilities within laws and regulations to give residents of Diemen, such as young people who want to stay here, a better chance of obtaining housing.

112294.explanation: The Diemen VVD stands for a clean future; we look at a mix of energy sources, including solar energy.

112296.explanation: The Diemen VVD finds sport important for everyone. Space is limited and we see no possibilities for a new sports park in Diemen South.

112298.explanation: The Diemen VVD is cautious when it comes to allowing coffeeshops. It could lead to more crime in our neighborhoods, more illegal cannabis cultivation, and increased police deployment.

112301.explanation: The Diemen VVD believes it is important that houses are built faster and in greater numbers. We also look at new initiatives such as temporary housing, flexible housing, shared housing, and simplifying room rental, so young people have more opportunities to live in Diemen.

112302.explanation: The Diemen VVD values connection and meeting people. Whether each neighborhood needs its own community center should be assessed per neighborhood.

112303.explanation: The Diemen VVD finds sufficient parking at shopping centers essential. We make agreements with the owner of the Diemerplein shopping center to keep parking rates as low as possible.

112304.explanation: The Diemen VVD finds sufficient parking at shopping centers essential. For this, an inventory of the number of parking spaces is necessary and we invest where needed.

112306.explanation: The Diemen VVD considers an up-to-date town hall important, also as an employer. The Diemen VVD believes the municipality should manage money carefully and wisely.

112307.explanation: Everyone must feel safe in Diemen – at home, on the street, at school, and in the neighborhood. Everyone, including women, must always be able to move safely on the streets, even at night. Lighting is important. Also cameras and increased enforcement.

112251.explanation: We support more resident participation. Whether this should be exclusively via a referendum is debatable. Experimentation is allowed.

112252.explanation: Maintenance of parks and green spaces is already done by residents. For a sports hall, it is more complicated and higher requirements apply (e.g., hygiene and safety).

112256.explanation: Given the large space requirements, there is no room for distribution centers in Diemen.

112258.explanation: Diemen contributes to the reception of asylum seekers, as all other municipalities must do (Distribution Law). For safety reasons, only families, women, and LGBTI individuals may be accommodated.

112260.explanation: Only if fireplaces and stoves have facilities to capture emissions of dust and soot.

112262.explanation: We will try to do that in 2027.

112268.explanation: The municipality passes on the money it receives from the national government for this to the local broadcaster.

112269.explanation: All school buildings have been renovated or newly built in recent years. Only the Sint-Petrus school needs to be renewed. The council has allocated a starting budget for this.

112273.explanation: "More enforcement by police and BOAs in the neighborhood." (Election program 2026-30)

112274.explanation: "Camera surveillance is only allowed if it is necessary and effective." (Election program 2026-30)

112275.explanation: Abuse of women, children, and the elderly also occurs in Diemen. We are open to an initiative to establish a shelter against domestic violence in Diemen.

112278.explanation: When elderly people move to smaller and more suitable housing, they leave behind a large home suitable for starting families.

112280.explanation: The so-called counter-performance is not mandatory in Diemen.

112282.explanation: The current limit of 130% of the statutory minimum income is sufficient.

112284.explanation: For every charging station for electric cars, 2 public parking spaces disappear.

112285.explanation: "Maximum speed limit of 30 km/h in built-up areas. The municipality must implement traffic measures to reduce speeds on roads where more than 40 km/h is currently possible." (Election program 2026-30)

112286.explanation: Although we can understand the desire, fulfilling it is not realistic in the current times of staff shortages in healthcare.

112287.explanation: It concerns the price/quality ratio of sometimes very specialized care where no cheaper alternatives are available.

112290.explanation: The Diemen tourist tax is very low compared to that of Amsterdam.

112292.explanation: "Housing is preferably allocated to local home seekers." (Election program 2026-30). The problem is that there are too few social rental homes. The best solution is: more building to shorten waiting times. Additionally, more lodging in private homes, additions, and flexible housing.

112294.explanation: If an owner of agricultural land applies for a permit to place solar panels on their land, the municipality must grant that permit if all conditions are met.

112296.explanation: Where is there space in Diemen South?

112298.explanation: Since 1999, the municipality has implemented a so-called zero option policy for coffeeshops. No permits are issued. Under strict conditions and with good organization, a coffeeshop could be allowed. In 2018, we organized a council excursion to the coffeeshop in Bussum.

112301.explanation: We are against converting rental homes into multiple rooms because it often benefits only the landlord and not the residents, tenants, or home seekers.

112302.explanation: Most neighborhoods already have a community center or neighborhood room. Only Holland Park and Biesbosch still need their own community centers.

112303.explanation: We support free parking for disabled people.

112304.explanation: There are enough parking spaces for cars in the center. However, there is a shortage of bicycle parking facilities.

112306.explanation: The municipality missed an opportunity by not installing high-pressure toilets during the renovation. That could have saved approximately 1 million liters of drinking water per year.

112307.explanation: That applies to all bicycle paths and sidewalks.

112251.explanation: Residents should be able to voice their opinions on important decisions. This increases trust and leads to better decision-making. The municipal council must take these signals seriously.

112252.explanation: The municipality must remain responsible for public services. This ensures quality, safety, and equal treatment in all neighborhoods.

112256.explanation: can be located in the business park and generates income for the municipality and employment.

112258.explanation: Diemen already has high housing pressure and little space. The municipality must first provide sufficient housing and facilities for its own residents. Shelter must be fairly distributed across the Netherlands, in line with the scale and capacity of municipalities.

112260.explanation: Nuisance must be prevented, but a total ban is not always necessary. Targeted measures and enforcement in case of nuisance are more effective and easier to implement.

112262.explanation: A central fireworks display ensures a safe and festive New Year's Eve and reduces damage and nuisance.

112264.explanation: Once a year is acceptable and it closes at a reasonable time.

112268.explanation: Local media provide transparency and keep residents engaged with their municipality. This is essential for a well-functioning local democracy.

112269.explanation: Children deserve good and safe school buildings. This is a core task of the municipality.

112273.explanation: More enforcement officers are needed to tackle nuisance and insecurity. Visible enforcement increases safety and confidence in the municipality.

112274.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent and resolve crime and nuisance. Its use must be targeted and careful.

112275.explanation: Diemen is too small; the location becomes quickly known, which is not conducive to safety.

112278.explanation: More suitable housing for the elderly is necessary. This helps seniors live independently and promotes housing turnover.

112280.explanation: A reciprocal obligation helps people stay engaged and can contribute to returning to work, provided it fits the personal situation.

112282.explanation: The municipality must take the actual cost of living into account. This way, more residents can receive timely support.

112284.explanation: The municipality must facilitate sufficient charging options, with a good balance in public space.

112285.explanation: Main roads should maintain the current speed. Small through roads of 30 km/h or even 15 km/h are fine.

112286.explanation: We do not have authority over that. The healthcare provider/insurer is responsible for that.

112287.explanation: The municipality must handle public funds carefully. Good care must remain affordable and accessible.

112290.explanation: Visitors thus contribute to municipal services. A reduction would mean residents would have to pay more.

112292.explanation: In times of housing shortage, allocation of housing must be fair. Residents who have been waiting a long time should not fall further behind.

112294.explanation: We do not have authority over that.

112296.explanation: With Diemen’s growth, expansion of sports facilities is necessary to provide space for clubs.

112298.explanation: This must be carefully assessed, with attention to safety, livability, and support.

112302.explanation: Community centers strengthen livability and social cohesion.

112303.explanation: Parking must remain affordable for residents and entrepreneurs. This is important for accessibility and a lively center.

112304.explanation: Diemen’s growth requires sufficient parking spaces to prevent nuisance.

112307.explanation: Good lighting increases safety and prevents unsafe situations.

statements.title: The Zilvermeeuwpad should get better lighting.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents of Diemen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Diemen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Diemen should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The use of fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed in Diemen.

titleParts.text: In Diemen moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Diemen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
More small rental and owner-occupied homes should be built for seniors in Diemen.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Diemen should establish a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Every neighborhood in Diemen should get its own community center.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Diemen should build a new sports park in Diemen-Zuid.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should invest more money in sustainability when renovating the town hall.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet bij de verbouwing van het gemeentehuis meer geld steken in

titleParts.glossary: When you can use (or reuse) something for a long time.
